sections.title: Part 1
sections.passage: You will hear a conversation between a park ranger and a teaching assistant about organising educational visits to Hinchingbrooke Country Park.

questionGroups.title: Hinchingbrooke Country Park
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

content.text:
**The park**
Area:

content.question: Area: ______ hectares

content.text:
hectares
Habitats: wetland, grassland and woodland
Wetland: lakes, ponds and a

content.question: Wetland: lakes, ponds and a ______

content.text:
Wildlife includes birds, insects and animals

**Subjects studied in educational visits include**
Science: Children look at

content.question: Science: Children look at ______ about plants, etc.

content.text:
about plants, etc.
Geography: includes learning to use a

content.question: Geography: includes learning to use a ______ and compass

content.text:
and compass
History: changes in land use
Leisure and tourism: mostly concentrates on the park's

content.question: Leisure and tourism: mostly concentrates on the park's ______

content.text: Music: Children make

content.question: Music: Children make ______ with natural materials

content.text:
with natural materials, and experiment with rhythm and speed.

**Benefits of outdoor educational visits**
They give children a feeling of

content.question: They give children a feeling of ______ that they may not have elsewhere

content.text:
that they may not have elsewhere.
Children learn new

content.question: Children learn new ______ and gain self-confidence

content.text:
and gain self-confidence.

**Practical issues**
Cost per child: £

content.question: Cost per child: £______

content.text: Adults, such as

content.question: Adults, such as ______, free

content.text: , free

sections.title: Part 2
sections.passage: You will hear a talk about the Stanthorpe Twining Association and plans for a visit from a French group.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: During the visit to Malatte, in France, members especially enjoyed
What will happen in Stanthorpe to mark the 25th anniversary of the Twining Association?

options.text: A garden seat will be bought.

questions.question: Which event raised most funds this year?
For the first evening with the French visitors host families are advised to

options.text: take them for a walk round the town.

questions.question: On Saturday evening there will be the chance to

questionGroups.instructions: Label the map below. Write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 16–20.

questions.question: The Temple of the Four Winds

sections.title: Part 3
sections.passage: You will hear two students, Colin and a female student, discussing Colin's project on reusing waste bread and other food-related topics.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO things did Colin find most satisfying about his bread reuse project?

options.text: finding a good way to prevent waste
overcoming problems in a basic process

questionGroups.question: Which TWO ways do the students agree that touch-sensitive sensors for food labels could be developed in future?

options.text: to show that food is no longer fit to eat
for use with drinks as well as foods
to provide applications for blind people
to indicate the weight of certain foods

questionGroups.instructions: What is the students' opinion about each of the following food trends? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 25–30.

options.text: This is only relevant to young people.
This already seems to be widespread.
Retailers should do more to encourage this.
More financial support is needed for this.
Most people know little about this.
There should be stricter regulations about this.

questions.question: Use of branded products related to celebrity chefs
Development of 'ghost kitchens' for takeaway food
Use of mushrooms for common health concerns

sections.title: Part 4
sections.passage: You will hear a lecture about the Céide Fields, an important Neolithic archaeological site in the northwest of Ireland.

questionGroups.title: Céide Fields
questionGroups.instructions: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

content.text:
● an important Neolithic archaeological site in the northwest of Ireland

**Discovery**
● In the 1930s, a local teacher realised that stones beneath the bog surface were once

content.question: stones beneath the bog surface were once ______

content.text:
.
● His

content.question: His ______ became an archaeologist

content.text:
became an archaeologist and undertook an investigation of the site:
   - a traditional method used by local people to dig for

content.question: a traditional method used to dig for ______

content.text:
was used to identify where stones were located
   - carbon dating later proved the site was Neolithic.
● Items are well preserved in the bog because of a lack of

content.question: Items are well preserved because of a lack of ______

content.text:
.

**Neolithic farmers**
● Houses were

content.question: Houses were ______ in shape

content.text:
in shape and had a hole in the roof.
● Neolithic innovations include:
   - cooking indoors
   - pots used for storage and to make

content.question: pots used for storage and to make ______

content.text:
.
● Each field at Céide was large enough to support a big

content.question: Each field was large enough to support a big ______

content.text:
.
● The fields were probably used to restrict the grazing of animals – no evidence of structures to house them during

content.question: no evidence of structures to house them during ______

content.text:
.

**Reasons for the decline in farming**
● a decline in

content.question: a decline in ______ quality

content.text:
quality
● an increase in

content.question: an increase in ______
